speaker: Susan
content: I didn't realize anybody was going to be out here. I just sort of rolled out of bed.
mark: was going to 过去将来时 would + 动词原形 / was/were going to + 动词原形
roll out of bed （睡眼惺忪地）从床上翻身起来，一骨碌从床上爬起来

speaker: Mike
content: I'm sure you look fine. Oh, Bongo, stop. Sorry, he scares easy.
mark: he scares easy 他很容易受到惊吓

speaker: Susan
content: No, it's fine. I get it.

speaker: Mike
content: I didn't mean to disturb you. I'll see you later.
mark: mean to 打算，计划
disturb v. 打扰，妨碍；使焦虑，使烦恼；移动，弄乱

speaker: Susan
content: Do you want to have dinner with me?

speaker: Mike
mark: Just the two of us (the可以省略) 就我们两个

speaker: Susan
content: Well, and Julie. It's a thing we do when somebody new moves into the neighborhood, we invite them over for a home-cooked meal. Sort of a tradition.
mark: It's a thing we do when... 这是我们在…时会做的事
home-cooked meal 家常菜
sort of a tradition 算是一种传统
sort of 有点儿，有几分;口语化的表达

speaker: Mike
content: I thought you said you were a lousy cook.
mark: lousy adj. 差劲的；不擅长的

speaker: Susan
mark: takeout n. 外卖
order takeout 点外卖

speaker: Mike
content: You invite them over for home cooking and give them takeout?

speaker: Susan
content: Yeah, it's sort of a new tradition. I'm working out the kinks.
mark: I'm working out the kinks. 我正在解决小问题、磨合。实施新的事物时会遇到一些小问题或需要调整的地方，就会用到这个短语来表示自己正在努力解决这些状况，使事情能够顺利进行
work out 解决，算出，制定
kink n. 奇想；扭结；蜷缩

speaker: Mike
content: I'll tell you what. How about I cook? And you guys come over to my place. Friday night at six?
mark: I'll tell you what. 我有个主意
come over to my place 来我家
Friday night at six 周五晚上六点

speaker: Susan
content: Oh, great. I'll be there. By, Bongo.

speaker: Mike
content: Julie!  Mike Delfino just invited us to dinner Friday night.

speaker: Mike
content: But only I'm going because you're gonna come down with something semi-serious that requires bed rest and fluids.
mark: come down with 染上（疾病）
semi-serious 比较严重的（semi-: 部分的）
bed rest and fluids 卧床休息和补液

speaker: Alice
content: Julie was glad Susan was rekindling her love life. Of course, she was unaware of her mother's recent track record with fire.
mark: rekindle v. 重新点燃 kindle v. 点燃，开始燃烧；激起，激发；
be unaware of sth 没有意识到某事；不知道某事；未察觉到某事
track record with fire 纵火/惹火的前科
track record with 在…方面的过往表现、业绩记录、履历情况
